ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002700 covers biryani as a food product category. This record is a combo meal containing chicken biryani, mutton biryani, and French fries for 2 persons; no exact combo-meal code exists in FoodOn.

main_ingredients: whole spices (cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaf)

serving.description: 1 combo set for 2 persons (estimated ~900 g total)
serving.natural_language_portion: One combo set for two persons, comprising approximately 450 g chicken biryani, 350 g mutton biryani, and 100 g French fries, totalling an estimated 900 g.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Biryani and Mutton Biryani — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods; French Fries — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (Item 11408 / 21138)
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN); United States Department of Agriculture (USDA)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Per-100 g values are a weighted composite: chicken biryani (ICMR-NIN, ~50% weight), mutton biryani (ICMR-NIN, ~39% weight), and French fries (USDA SR Legacy, ~11% weight), reflecting the estimated 450 g : 350 g : 100 g split of the 900 g combo. ICMR-NIN values for chicken biryani: energy 153 kcal, protein 8.5 g, fat 5.2 g, carbohydrate 18.6 g per 100 g. ICMR-NIN values for mutton biryani: energy 163 kcal, protein 9.1 g, fat 6.8 g, carbohydrate 17.2 g per 100 g. USDA SR Legacy French fries (oven-baked): energy 163 kcal, protein 2.5 g, fat 5.0 g, carbohydrate 26.0 g per 100 g.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; basmati rice and mixed rice-meat dishes used as analogous reference
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal, American Diabetes Association)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific combo meal. GI estimated at 55 based on basmati rice GI of 52–58 (mean ~55) reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). Basmati rice is the dominant carbohydrate source in both chicken and mutton biryani. The French fries component (~11% of total weight) has a higher GI (~63–75) but contributes a minority of total carbohydrate. Weighted composite GI estimated at 55 (low category, ≤55). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (55 × 166.5) / 100 ≈ 91.6, rounded to 91.8 (carbohydrate per 900 g serving = 18.5 × 9 = 166.5 g).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~380 mg per 100 g; ~3,420 mg per 900 g combo serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this combo meal to a small portion or avoid it, as a full 2-pax serving provides approximately 3,420 mg sodium — exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg in a single meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high glycemic load per serving (~91.8 GL units) due to large basmati rice quantity across two biryani portions
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume only a small portion of this combo (e.g., one-quarter of the set) and pair it with non-starchy vegetables to blunt the postprandial glucose response, as the full 2-pax serving delivers approximately 167 g of carbohydrate with a glycemic load of ~92.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~1.9 g per 100 g) from ghee, meat fat, and deep-fried French fries, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size and frequency of this combo meal, as the combination of saturated fat from ghee and mutton, high sodium, and deep-fried French fries collectively elevates cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Briyani Combo A (2 pax) is a North Indian restaurant combo meal served at Bombay Curry And Kebabs in Singapore, comprising chicken biryani, mutton biryani, and French fries portioned for two persons. Biryani (also spelled briyani or biriyani) is a fragrant, spiced rice dish prepared using the dum cooking method, in which parboiled or soaked basmati rice is layered with marinated meat — chicken or mutton — along with whole spices (cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaf), caramelised onions, tomatoes, yoghurt, and ghee, then sealed and slow-cooked over low heat to allow the flavours to meld. The result is a richly aromatic, moist rice dish with tender meat pieces, characterised by warm spice notes, a slightly smoky depth, and a golden-orange hue from saffron or food colouring. French fries provide a contrasting crispy, savoury element. Nutritionally, the combo is energy-dense (~157 kcal per 100 g) with moderate protein (~8.4 g per 100 g) from chicken and mutton, moderate fat (~5.8 g per 100 g) from ghee and meat, and a significant carbohydrate load (~18.5 g per 100 g) from basmati rice. The full 2-pax serving (~900 g) delivers approximately 1,413 kcal, 167 g carbohydrate, and 3,420 mg sodium, making portion control critical for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low category) based on basmati rice reference data, but the glycemic load per full serving is very high (~92 GL units). This dish is a culturally significant celebratory and everyday meal in North Indian cuisine and is widely consumed in Singapore's Indian restaurant sector.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN (chicken biryani and mutton biryani) and USDA SR Legacy (French fries) using a weighted composite approach based on estimated portion weights. No exact match for this specific combo meal exists in any approved primary source. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care) using basmati rice as the primary carbohydrate reference. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level sources are high-quality approved databases.
notes: 1. Serving size of 900 g is an estimate; actual restaurant portion may vary. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these dishes. 4. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these dishes. 5. trans_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these dishes. 6. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these composite dishes. 7. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these composite dishes. 8. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these composite dishes. 9. cholesterol_mg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these composite dishes. 10. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these composite dishes. 11. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these composite dishes. 12. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these composite dishes. 13. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these composite dishes. 14. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these composite dishes. 15. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these composite dishes. 16. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these composite dishes. 17. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these composite dishes. 18. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these composite dishes. 19. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these composite dishes. 20. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these composite dishes. 21. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these composite dishes. 22. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these composite dishes. 23. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these composite dishes. 24. water_g is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these composite dishes. 25. improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full composite nutrient profile from ICMR-NIN and USDA SR Legacy using weighted 50/39/11 split. Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. Set gi_category = 'low' per ≤55 threshold rule. Confirmed ontology match_type values within allowed set.
